item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this a rough draft?

That's my impression of this. I guess this is why it's a miss for some people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one, I just wish it was fleshed out more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one just doesn't click for me at all. As the post by Narsil098 says it's a pariah from Warhammer 40k. That's it. A psychic blank that radiates an aura of disquiet due to it being a 'hole' in the background psychic potential. The lack of details almost seems like the Foundation doesn't care about this scip enough to properly catalogue and study it. -1 I'm afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice, Foundation has a Blank/Pariah.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
The one-two punch of this and it's Tale struck a great chord with me, I hope my reading of both do them justice. Thanks for the great story!

https://youtu.be/k3RQXvtJYec

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It indicates that the SCP in question is a hardcore gamer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WTF is a "Safe-exsequi"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Re: the hyphen designation, the author (Scantron/Communism will win) is known for using alternate Object Class in his SCPs (i.e. [Safe/Euclid/Keter]-[Latin word that implies the nature of the SCP]).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but shouldn't this be an Euclid? It's a live being, after all, you can't just leave him somewhere out of reach from others and be done with it. And I also think that that kind of hyphen designation has been deemed obsolete. Upvoted anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm aware that this is a bit of a "hit or miss" article, and it's most definitely a hit for me. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Came to the discussion thread to explain why I downvoted.
Read the comments.
Switched to an upvote.
I really dig the fact that this thing's mere presence is capable of causing mass paranoia and fear for unknown reasons. I'm not really scared of crowds but the mental image of being trampled underfoot by thousands of panicked screaming individuals while some anonymous entity just stands there and maybe laughs, while boring to me at first, now creeps me the fuck out after some time to think it over.

+Dumb theory
D-U-M dumb

What if he's not laughing? What if he's crying instead? Some sort of entity cursed him into a near vegetative state and caused him to be unable to mingle with others, totally isolating him from society. And he's depressed about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this article. It isn't overbearing with details, letting the reader have something to work with, yet isn't vague either. Shows that shorter articles can be just as sweet as their larger brethren.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i don't fucking get it

I finished reading this, and was like, this is dumb, plus it has an alternate class system which I think is dumb, so I scroll up to downvote. Then I see I miss the addendum, so I read that. And I'm like, "still dumb." But I'm like, "wait, I'm really creeped out right now." So yeah, I guess now I'm upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, unless I suddenly feel like it. Which isn't impossible, mind you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I noticed that it really resonates for some people and doesn't do anything for others. I kinda wonder what the deciding factor is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scantron, I have to say, this is my favorite of your articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. I don't know why this bothered me so much.

Are you going to be making a list of these suffixes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Means that it's capable of independent action. 'exsequi' is latin for "carry out".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Object Class: Safe-exsequi

I should be the last person to not want to see a new object class, but I just don't get this part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks :) No idea about Left 4 Dead stuff, though. I don't video games.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And Scantron does it again. Nice work!

For some reason it reminds me of the Screamer that was cut from Left 4 Dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's okay, no big deal. And you know what? I kind of agree with the points you made.

I've changed my mind. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd rather leave his exact personal characteristics unsaid, because ultimately, the unknown is scarier than the known. It also doesn't feel right for the personification of a faceless, uncaring mob to have so many physical characteristics defined.

I'm not going to change the effect. It's short, simple, well-received, and reasonably highly rated. Even more important, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What is his approximate age, height and weight? His panic aura doesn't affect people immediatly, so personnel should have time to measure these characteristics. Also, even if his exact ethnicy is undetermined, you can still say what color his skin is.

I personally don't have any phobia of crowds, so it doesn't scare me much,though admittedly, the hundreds of people pushing, beating and yelling obscenites at each other, even if they were best friends before, is a very disturbing mental image. He could be made scarier if, for example, his panic aura sometimes changed into/was also a humiliation aura (people are much more likely to commit embarassing mistakes that draw lots of attention themselves, and onlookers are more likely to make fun of and film these mistakes with their cell phones to put them on the Internet, for example).

The article is written in a clinical tone and the containment procedures are reasonable, so no vote for now. It has potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, the "may or may not be laughter" part? It totally sells it for me.

It gives the impression that the SCP could damn well be totally sentient, and is playing the Foundation in order to be able to be part of more tests, to sate it's thirst for the hysteria it causes.

Of course, I could be reading too much into that, but there you go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That wouldn't be outside the realm of possibility, but I personally prefer it as a stand-alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This could almost be a Little Mister, such as Mr. Panic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This so much, that gave me the shivers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I don't care for the laughter part, but saying it 'may or may not be laughter' saves that a bit.

Besides that, ChazzK put it very well. I like this quite a bit, and upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's why I like this SCP despite not having any type of social phobia myself: it doesn't do a damn thing, just stands there, maybe wanders aimlessly, and just confusedly watches and/or laughs as *everyone else* goes bananas.

Oh sure an SCP that suddenly looms up hugely and directly scares the bajeebus out of people is more immediately frightening, but someone whose mere presence drives groups of people into a bloody panic and they don't even know why is a real psycho-thrill to me.

+1 good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do still wonder how people might react if they notice the SCP, unless one of its features is that it remains unnoticed in the crowd.

I imagine they'd think "hey, why is that person so calm? And where are they going?"

And then they'd get trampled to death while 1268 laughs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Being familiar with Social Anxiety myself, and the fear of crowds, this definitely hits home with the fear factor. However, I do still wonder how people might react if they notice the SCP, unless one of its features is that it remains unnoticed in the crowd. Aside from that, good work utilizing mass panic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What made it creepy is that it laughs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. From what I've been hearing, it's a hit or miss sort of thing, so you either like it or you don't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brevity is the soul of terror.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get anything from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scantron, you are amazing, because even with the terse report, I now have tears in my eyes from fear.

Everything about crowds that scares me is in here.

Bravo, Scantron.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the Ethics committee would like a word with you.

EDIT: And I replied to the wrong person. Welp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would certainly be a (fun) possibility if it weren't for the fact that there are more efficient and reliable ways of killing a bunch of people. Plus, cross-contamination etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I say we send him into a room of handcuffed D-Class instead of terminating them at the end-of-the-month.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice, Scantron.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gah, one of my fears is to be crushed in a stampede of panicked people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was inspired by my fear of crowds and someone mentioning the name "Waldo" in chat. I knew right then that I was going to write that SCP.
